title: Get Started with rsgl
text: SGL (Structured Graphics Language) declarative language generating statistical graphics relational data. designed feel like SQL — can write SELECT statement, can write SGL statement. rsgl implements SGL R package. pass SGL statement DuckDB connection dbGetPlot(), returns ggplot2 plot object.

title: Get Started with rsgl
text: Install rsgl GitHub: Load package create DuckDB connection data: cars table now contains mtcars dataset columns like hp (horsepower), mpg (miles per gallon), cyl (cylinders), wt (weight).
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("sgl-projects/rsgl") library(rsgl) library(duckdb) #> Loading required package: DBI  con <- dbConnect(duckdb()) dbWriteTable(con, "cars", mtcars)

title: Get Started with rsgl
text: SGL statement three required parts: visualize — maps columns visual properties (aesthetics) — specifies data source using — chooses geometric object (geom)  maps hp x-axis mpg y-axis, pulls data cars table, draws point row.
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y   from cars   using points ")

title: Get Started with rsgl
text: Map third column color aesthetic distinguish groups:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y,     cyl as color   from cars   using points ")

title: Get Started with rsgl
text: Swap points different geom change representation. Use bars bar chart — combined count(*) count rows per group:  Use line line chart, boxes box plots:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     cyl_cat as x,     count(*) as y   from (     select cast(cyl as varchar) as cyl_cat     from cars   )   group by     cyl_cat   using bars ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     cyl_cat as x,     mpg as y   from (     select mpg, cast(cyl as varchar) as cyl_cat     from cars   )   using boxes ")

title: Get Started with rsgl
text: Combine multiple geoms layer keyword. overlays regression line scatterplot:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y   from cars   using (     points     layer     regression line   ) ")

title: Get Started with rsgl
text: SGL Language Guide covers full syntax including transformations (bin, count), grouping, collection, scaling, faceting, coordinate systems, titles.

title: SGL Language Guide
text: examples use dbGetPlot(), takes DuckDB connection SGL statement. ’ll load datasets -memory DuckDB database work .
code: library(rsgl) library(duckdb) #> Loading required package: DBI  con <- dbConnect(duckdb()) dbWriteTable(con, "cars", mtcars) dbWriteTable(con, "trees", as.data.frame(Orange))  diamonds <- ggplot2::diamonds diamonds$cut <- as.character(diamonds$cut) diamonds$color <- as.character(diamonds$color) diamonds$clarity <- as.character(diamonds$clarity) dbWriteTable(con, "diamonds", diamonds)

title: SGL Language Guide
text: SGL statement built clauses. minimal statement three: visualize, , using. Additional clauses control grouping, scaling, faceting, titles. statement ends semicolon (optional passed string dbGetPlot()).
code: visualize   <aesthetic mappings> from <data source> [group by <grouping expressions>] [collect by <collection expressions>] using <geom expression> [scale by <scale expressions>] [facet by <facet expressions>] [title <title expressions>]

title: SGL Language Guide
text: clause specifies data source. typically table name:  SQL subquery can used filtering transforming data visualization. subquery must enclosed parentheses:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y   from cars   using points ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y   from (     select hp, mpg     from cars     where hp < 200   )   using points ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: visualize clause maps data source columns aesthetics. mapping form <column> <aesthetic>.

title: SGL Language Guide
text: Cartesian coordinates use x y:  Polar coordinates use theta (angle) r (radius) — see coordinate systems section examples. single positional aesthetic also valid:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y   from cars   using points ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     mpg as x   from cars   using points ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: color maps column color:  size maps column point size:  Multiple aesthetics can combined:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y,     cyl as color   from cars   using points ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y,     cyl as size   from cars   using points ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y,     cyl as color,     wt as size   from cars   using points ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: Aesthetics can mapped expressions include transformations aggregations, just bare column names. supported column-level transformations aggregations covered section .

title: SGL Language Guide
text: using clause specifies geometric object (geom) represents data. singular plural forms accepted keywords.

title: SGL Language Guide
text: points (point) draws individual points — one per row:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y   from cars   using points ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: bars (bar) draws bar segments. Bars stacked default non-positional grouping present:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     cut as x,     count(*) as y,     color as color   from diamonds   group by     cut, color   using bars ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: lines (line) draws connected lines. Lines collective geoms — represent multiple rows single geometric object:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     age as x,     circumference as y   from trees   collect by     Tree   using lines ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: boxes (box) draws box plots. Like lines, boxes collective geoms:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     cut as x,     price as y   from diamonds   using boxes ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: SGL supports transformations aggregations part column expressions visualize clause.

title: SGL Language Guide
text: bin() transformation groups continuous column discrete bins:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     bin(mpg) as x,     count(*) as y   from cars   group by     bin(mpg)   using bars ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: count(*) aggregation counts rows per group. non-aggregated column visualize clause must also appear group clause — rule SQL:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     cut as x,     count(*) as y   from diamonds   group by     cut   using bars ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: bin() count(*) can combined produce histograms. Non-positional groupings create stacked histograms:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     bin(mpg) as x,     count(*) as y,     cyl_cat as color   from (     select       *,       cast(cyl as varchar) as cyl_cat     from cars   )   group by     bin(mpg),     cyl_cat   using bars ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: group clause specifies grouping expressions aggregations. follows semantics SQL’s GROUP : non-aggregated expression visualize clause must appear group clause.
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     cut as x,     count(*) as y   from diamonds   group by     cut   using bars ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: Collective geoms (lines boxes) represent multiple rows single geometric object. default, collection rows determined implicitly. collect clause overrides behavior, explicitly specifying column determines rows grouped separate geometric objects. Without collect , rows feed single line:  collect , unique value specified column produces separate line:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     age as x,     circumference as y   from trees   using line ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     age as x,     circumference as y   from trees   collect by     Tree   using lines ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: Geom qualifiers keywords placed geom name using clause. modify geom represents data.

title: SGL Language Guide
text: Adds random positional variation points, useful data overlapping values:
code: set.seed(42) dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     cyl_cat as x,     mpg as y   from (     select mpg, cast(cyl as varchar) as cyl_cat     from cars   )   using jittered points ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: Fits linear regression line instead connecting data points:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y   from cars   using regression line ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: Positions bars side--side instead stacking :
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     cut as x,     count(*) as y,     color as color   from diamonds   group by     cut, color   using unstacked bars ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: Multiple layers geometric objects can combined single graphic using layer keyword. layer complete sub-statement visualize, , using clauses:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y   from cars   using points    layer    visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y   from cars   using regression line ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: layers share data source aesthetic mapping, shorthand syntax avoids repetition. Geom expressions listed inside parentheses using clause, separated layer:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y   from cars   using (     points     layer     regression line   ) ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: mapped aesthetic scale determines data values mapped visual properties. Scales default linear can overridden scale clause.

title: SGL Language Guide
text: log() applies base-10 logarithmic scale aesthetic:  Note scaling applied aesthetic, data. means regression example computed log-scaled values. transform data , use SQL clause:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y   from cars   using (     points     layer     regression line   )   scale by     log(x),     log(y) ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     log_hp as x,     log_mpg as y   from (     select       log(hp) as log_hp,       log(mpg) as log_mpg     from cars   )   using (     points     layer     regression line   ) ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: coordinate system inferred positional aesthetics visualize clause. x y imply Cartesian coordinates. theta r imply polar coordinates. pie chart stacked bar chart rendered polar coordinates:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     count(*) as theta,     cut as color   from diamonds   group by     cut   using bars ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: Faceting generates small multiples — separate panels unique value column. single facet expression produces horizontal panels default:  orientation can changed vertically keyword:  Two facet expressions produce grid — one varies horizontally, vertically:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y   from cars   using points   facet by     cyl ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y   from cars   using points   facet by     cyl vertically ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y   from cars   using points   facet by     cyl,     am ")

title: SGL Language Guide
text: Axis legend titles automatically derived aesthetic mappings. title clause overrides defaults:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y,     cyl as color   from cars   using points   title     x as 'Horsepower',     y as 'Miles Per Gallon',     color as 'Cylinders' ")

title: Authors and Citation
text: Jon Chapman. Author, maintainer.

title: rsgl Documentation Plan
text: README.Rmd: Minimal — one paragraph, installation, one basic scatterplot example man/dbGetPlot.Rd: Auto-generated, bare-minimum description single exported function vignettes, pkgdown config, package-level help page (?rsgl)

title: rsgl Documentation Plan
text: rsgl exactly one exported R function (dbGetPlot). thing users actually need learn SGL language — clauses, keywords, grammar. SGL paper covers rigorously academic paper, practical guide R users. documentation needs bridge gap.

title: rsgl Documentation Plan
text: README first thing users see GitHub pkgdown site. current version sparse orient someone. expanded include: one-sentence pitch: SGL exists (SQL-like language specifying statistical graphics) 2-3 example SGL statements rendered plot output, showing variety (scatterplot, histogram, something layers facets) visitors immediately grasp language’s expressiveness Installation instructions (already present) link SGL paper Pointers “Get Started” vignette language guide stays concise — ’s landing page, tutorial.

title: rsgl Documentation Plan
text: introductory vignette new users. pkgdown use “Get Started” tab ’s named rsgl.Rmd. Contents: SGL — brief, non-academic explanation. SGL graphics language designed feel like SQL. write declarative statement, rsgl parses , queries DuckDB database, returns ggplot2 plot. Setup — installing package, creating DuckDB connection, loading example data (cars trees tables used paper good canonical examples) first plot — walkthrough simple scatterplot statement, breaking clause (visualize, , using) Adding aesthetics — mapping third column color Changing geoms — switching points bars/lines go next — links language guide gallery short enough user can follow 5-10 minutes.

title: rsgl Documentation Plan
text: comprehensive reference SGL syntax implemented rsgl. single important piece documentation. Organized clause, mirroring structure paper written practical documentation runnable R examples: clause — specifying tables SQL subqueries data sources visualize clause — mapping columns aesthetics (x, y, color, size, theta, r) using clause — geom types (points/point, bars/bar, lines/line, boxes/box) Column-level transformations aggregations — bin() count() column expressions, group clause collect clause — controlling collection behavior collective geoms (line, box) Geom qualifiers — jittered, regression, unstacked layer operator — combining multiple layers, including shorthand layered geom expression syntax scale clause — log(x), log(y), distinction scaling aesthetics vs. transforming data SQL Coordinate systems — Cartesian (x/y) vs. polar (theta/r), pie charts stacked bar charts polar coordinates facet clause — horizontal/vertical panels, grid faceting two expressions title clause — overriding default axis/legend titles section least one runnable example plot output. vignette long, ’s appropriate — ’s reference document.

title: rsgl Documentation Plan
text: visual cookbook organized users want create, language feature. entry SGL statement rendered plot, minimal explanation. Categories: Scatterplots — basic, colored group, regression line overlay, jittered Bar charts — simple counts, stacked, unstacked, grouped Histograms — using bin() + count() Line plots — single series, multiple series collect , regression lines Box plots — basic, grouped Pie charts — using polar coordinates Faceted plots — horizontal, vertical, grid Multi-layer plots — points + regression, combining different geoms Log-scaled plots — using scale SQL subqueries — filtering transforming data visualization serves inspiration copy-paste reference. Users looking “make histogram?” can find immediately.

title: rsgl Documentation Plan

what: An Implementation of the SGL Graphics Language
title: An Implementation of the SGL Graphics Language
text: rsgl implements SGL (Structured Graphics Language), declarative language specifying statistical graphics designed feel like SQL. write SGL statement, pass dbGetPlot() DuckDB connection, get back ggplot2 plot.

title: An Implementation of the SGL Graphics Language
text: can install rsgl GitHub :
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("sgl-projects/rsgl")

title: An Implementation of the SGL Graphics Language
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y   from cars   using (     points     layer     regression line   )   scale by     log(x),     log(y) ")

code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     cyl_cat as x,     mpg as y   from (     select mpg, cast(cyl as varchar) as cyl_cat, am     from cars   )   using boxes   facet by     am ")

what: Generate a plot from a SGL statement — dbGetPlot
title: Generate a plot from a SGL statement — dbGetPlot
text: dbGetPlot takes DuckDB connection SGL statement returns plot defined SGL statement.

title: Generate a plot from a SGL statement — dbGetPlot
code: dbGetPlot(con, sgl_stmt)

title: Generate a plot from a SGL statement — dbGetPlot
text: con DuckDB connection created DBI::dbConnect() sgl_stmt SGL statement string defines plot

title: Generate a plot from a SGL statement — dbGetPlot
text: ggplot2 plot object

title: Generate a plot from a SGL statement — dbGetPlot
text: SGL statement parsed internal graphics structure, referenced data queried DuckDB, semantic validation performed, column-level transformations aggregations applied, result converted ggplot2 plot object. SGL statements support following clauses: visualize (aesthetic mappings), (data source SQL subquery), using (geom type), group , collect , scale , facet , title, layer operator combining multiple layers.

title: Generate a plot from a SGL statement — dbGetPlot
code: library(duckdb) #> Loading required package: DBI con <- dbConnect(duckdb()) dbWriteTable(con, "cars", mtcars)  # Scatterplot dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y   from cars   using points ")   # Histogram dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     bin(mpg) as x,     count(*) as y   from cars   group by     bin(mpg)   using bars ")   # Scatterplot with regression line overlay dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     hp as x,     mpg as y   from cars   using (     points     layer     regression line   ) ")

what: rsgl: An Implementation of the SGL Graphics Language — rsgl-package
title: rsgl: An Implementation of the SGL Graphics Language — rsgl-package
text: Parses executes SGL (Structured Graphics Language) statements 'DuckDB' databases produce 'ggplot2' visualizations. SGL declarative language generating statistical graphics relational data.

title: rsgl Implementation Plan
text: Change Package: sglref → Package: rsgl Change Title: something like Implementation SGL Graphics Language CRAN rule: title must Title Case, must redundantly start package name “package …” Change Description: proper paragraph (least one sentence): Fix typo: "packagee" → "package" (description text won’t present, verify) Change URL: https://github.com/sgl-projects/rsgl Change BugReports: https://github.com/sgl-projects/rsgl/issues Fix inconsistent indentation (sglref mixes tabs spaces) — use spaces Consider updating version 0.1.0 (first public release)
code: Parses and executes SGL (Structured Graphics Language) statements against 'DuckDB' databases to produce 'ggplot2' visualizations. SGL is a declarative language for generating statistical graphics from relational data. See Chapman (2025) <doi:10.48550/arXiv.2505.14690> for the language specification.
